perturbation.original_text: 1. “Proprietary Information,” as used herein, shall mean any and all samples, formula, methods, know-how, technology, software, mate rial, engineering data, specifications, sketches, drawings, schematics, designs, manufacturing processes, test results, compilations, and any other material, informa tion, ideas, concepts or knowledge which a party (the “Disclosing Party”) furnishes to a nother party (the “Receiving Party”): (i) in written or other tangible form whether marked w ith a proprietary legend or not, or (ii) in oral or visual form that is identified as proprietary at the time of disclosure and is summarized and designated proprietary in a written memorandum delivered to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of the disclosur e. Examples of such Proprietary Information include, but are not limited to, pricing, computer programs, computer code, modules, scripts, algorithms, features, and modes of operation, inventions (whether or not patentable), techniques, processes, methodologies, know-how, schematics, testing procedures, design and function specifications, documentation, and the features, mode of operation and other details of Disclosing Pa rty’s products and services, as well as the names and expertise of Disclosing Party’s employees, product development plans and forecasts.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. “Proprietary Information,” as used herein, shall mean any and all samples, formula, methods, know-how, technology, software, mate rial, engineering data, specifications, sketches, drawings, schematics, designs, manufacturing processes, test results, compilations, and any other material, informa tion, ideas, concepts or knowledge which a party (the “Disclosing Party”) furnishes to a nother party (the “Receiving Party”): (i) in written or other tangible form whether marked w ith a proprietary legend or not, or (ii) in oral or visual form that is identified as proprietary at the time of disclosure. Examples of such Proprietary Information include, but are not limited to, pricing, computer programs, computer code, modules, scripts, algorithms, features, and modes of operation, inventions (whether or not patentable), techniques, processes, methodologies, know-how, schematics, testing procedures, design and function specifications, documentation, and the features, mode of operation and other details of Disclosing Pa rty’s products and services, as well as the names and expertise of Disclosing Party’s employees, product development plans and forecasts.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement that oral or visual disclosures be summarized in a written memorandum within 30 days to be considered proprietary, we create a contradiction with later sections that rely on the written documentation to enforce confidentiality. This change impacts section 2 because it is no longer required to have proprietary information summarized and designated as proprietary in a written memorandum delivered to Receiving Party within thirty (30) days of the disclosur e.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement, unless disclosure or further use is authorized or consented to in writing by Disclosing Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or dela yed and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure). Such consent to disclose Proprietary Information to employees of Receiving Party or its affiliated company (“affiliated Receiving Party”) with a legitimate “need to know” and only for the purposes described in this Agreement is herewith given, but further consent shall be required for disclosure to others or authorization of use by others. For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" mean s and includes a parent, if any, of Receiving Party and all present and future companies in whic h Receiving Party, or its parent, individually or collectively, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, owns or controls fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding stock having the right to vote for or appoint directors thereof. Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for oral or visual disclosures to be summarized in a written memorandum within 30 days to be considered proprietary information. The contradicted text (section 2) relies on the definition of Proprietary Information, which was altered. Because it is no longer required to have proprietary information summarized and designated as proprietary in a written memorandum, Section 2's requirement for confidentiality of proprietary information becomes difficult to enforce for oral disclosures, thus creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement, unless disclosure or further use is authorized or consented to in writing by Disclosing Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or dela yed and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure). Such consent to disclose Proprietary Information to employees of Receiving Party or its affiliated company (“affiliated Receiving Party”) with a legitimate “need to know” and only for the purposes described in this Agreement is herewith given, but further consent shall be required for disclosure to others or authorization of use by others. For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" mean s and includes a parent, if any, of Receiving Party and all present and future companies in whic h Receiving Party, or its parent, individually or collectively, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, owns or controls fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding stock having the right to vote for or appoint directors thereof. Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement. Such consent to disclose Proprietary Information to employees of Receiving Party or its affiliated company (“affiliated Receiving Party”) with a legitimate “need to know” and only for the purposes described in this Agreement is herewith given, but further consent shall be required for disclosure to others or authorization of use by others. For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" mean s and includes a parent, if any, of Receiving Party and all present and future companies in whic h Receiving Party, or its parent, individually or collectively, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, owns or controls fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding stock having the right to vote for or appoint directors thereof. Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'unless disclosure or further use is authorized or consented to in writing by Disclosing Party which consent shall not be unreasonably withheld or dela yed and/or (ii) the case where disclosure of the same is required under applicable law or by a gove rnmental order, rule or regulation or by the regulations of any relevant stock exchange or other governmental authority (provided that the Receiving party shall give written notice of such re quired disclosure to the other party prior to the disclosure)' from Section 2, the Receiving Party is now strictly bound to only use Proprietary Information for purposes described in the agreement, regardless of legal obligations or written consent. This change impacts section 8 which talks about the law being in accordance to Delaware, where the change in section 2, goes against Delaware laws, leading to a contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles. Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be filed in the courts of Delaware.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 2 allows for disclosure or use of Proprietary Information under certain circumstances (written consent by Disclosing Party, legal requirements). The changed text removes these exceptions, creating a strict obligation to only use Proprietary Information for purposes described in the agreement. This directly contradicts Section 8, which states the agreement is governed by Delaware law. Delaware law might require disclosure in some cases, which would now be a breach of contract under the changed Section 2, creating a direct conflict and a strong contradiction. The change narrows down the acceptable circumstances for using proprietary information, which may cause a violation of Delaware laws, which section 8 abides by.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Immediately upon a request by the Disclosing Pa rty at any time, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Inform ation of the Disclosing Party and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Inform ation and any and all copies or extracts thereof or upon request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that all materials containing Proprietary Information (incl uding all copies thereof) have been destroyed. The Receiving Party understands th at nothing herein (a) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, which shall be disclosed if at all sole ly at the option of the Disclosing Party, or (b) requires the Disclosing Pa rty to proceed with any proposed transaction or relationship in connection with which Propr ietary Information may be disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Upon the termination of this agreement, the Receiving Party will turn over to the Disclosing Party all Proprietary Inform ation of the Disclosing Party and all documents or media containing any such Proprietary Inform ation and any and all copies or extracts thereof or upon request of the Disclosing Party, the Receiving Party shall certify in writing that all materials containing Proprietary Information (incl uding all copies thereof) have been destroyed. The Receiving Party understands th at nothing herein (a) requires the disclosure of any Proprietary Information of the Disclosing Party, which shall be disclosed if at all sole ly at the option of the Disclosing Party, or (b) requires the Disclosing Pa rty to proceed with any proposed transaction or relationship in connection with which Propr ietary Information may be disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Immediately upon a request by the Disclosing Party at any time' to 'Upon the termination of this agreement,' the Receiving Party is no longer obligated to return Proprietary Information during the agreement's term, creating a contradiction with the purpose of the agreement in the WHEREAS section which is about keeping safe confidential information from third parties. The change in section 4, therefore makes the original intent of the agreement questionable. The contradiction now applies to the section in the WHEREAS block.
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, TPI is a company that develops and manufactures large-scale composite materials for the wind energy, transportation and military markets; WHEREAS, TPI has developed valuable technology, know-how, trade secrets, and other confidential information relating to reinforced plastic composite materials and processes; WHEREAS, [Insert Company] desires to receive and examine certain confidential data of TPI relating to composite materials and/or other products that TPI has obtained, designed, manufactured or developed for use in the wind energy, transportation or military markets; WHEREAS, TPI is willing to provide [Insert Company] certain confidential and proprietary data relating to the above-re ferenced technology and processes; and WHEREAS, the Parties desire to define and set out the terms and conditions which prevent each of the Parties from any unlawful or unauthorized discl osure of confidential and pr oprietary information as stipulated hereinbelow.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed the Disclosing Party to request the return of Proprietary Information 'at any time'. The changed text limits this obligation to 'upon the termination of this agreement'. This creates a contradiction with the 'WHEREAS' section, which expresses the desire to prevent unauthorized disclosure of confidential information. The changed text now allows for potential unauthorized disclosure during the agreement's term, directly conflicting with the original intent stated in the WHEREAS clauses. This aligns with the definition of Omissions – In Text Contradiction, since the change removes the clause 'at any time' that was previously present in the original text.

perturbation.original_text: 6. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party sixty (60) days’ written notice. However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this Agreement. Neither Party shall assign this Ag reement to any third party, without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. This Non-Disclosure Agreement shall remain in full force and effect from the effective date indicated above. Either party may terminate this Agreement by giving the other Party sixty (60) days’ written notice. The Party shall assign this Ag reement to any third party, without the prior written consent of the other Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'However the confidentiality obligations and all powers, rights and duties provided herein shall extend for a period of five (5) years following the termination of this Agreement.' from Section 6, the confidentiality obligations of the agreement now cease upon termination, contradicting the intent in Section 7 that recognizes the unique nature of the information and the need for ongoing protection against irreparable harm. The contradiction affects Section 7, because section 7 has the intent for the Proprietary Information being protected, where section 6 is enabling ways for the Proprietary Information to not be protected.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. The Parties acknowledge and agree that due to the unique nature of the Disclosing Party’s Proprietary Information, money damages will not be a sufficient remedy for a breach of this Agreement. Because a violation of any of the provisions of this Agreement will likely cause irreparable loss and harm which cannot be reasonably or adequately compensated by damages in an action at law, the Disclosing Party will be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief or other appropriate equitable re lief as remedies for any such breach. Such remedies will not be deemed to be the exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement but will be in addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 6 removes the clause that extends confidentiality obligations beyond the termination of the agreement. This directly contradicts Section 7, which explicitly states that money damages are insufficient due to the unique nature of the proprietary information and anticipates ongoing protection against irreparable harm. The removal of the extended confidentiality term in Section 6 directly undermines the intent and enforceability of Section 7, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 8. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of Delaware, without regard to conflicts of law principles. Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be filed in the courts of Delaware.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. This Agreement shall be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of [Insert Company's State], without regard to conflicts of law principles. Any dispute, controversy and/or difference which may arise between the Parties out of or in relation to or in connection with this Agreement, or the breach thereof, which cannot be settled by amicable mutual accord without undue delay, shall be filed in the courts of [Insert Company's State].
perturbation.explanation: By changing the governing law from Delaware to [Insert Company's State], a contradiction arises if TPI's internal policies, referenced implicitly throughout the agreement, are compliant with Delaware law but not with [Insert Company's State] law. This is due to the fact that the document is by and between 'TPI Composites, Inc. a company established under the laws of Delaware', which makes it that Delaware's laws must be the one's being looked at throughout the agreement. The contradiction affects section 9, because in this section it is mentioned that the agreement is the entire agreement. However, the law being changed from Delaware to another state, can now include the state's laws, which makes section 9, a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreemen t of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and can cels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the Parties hereto relating to the Proprietary Information, whether written or oral, expressed or implied. The Parties agree that this Agreement is severable and that in the event any provision of this Agreement is held to be ille gal, invalid or enforceab le, the legality, validity and enforceability of the remaining provisi ons will not be affected or impaired.
perturbation.justification: The change in Section 8, which alters the governing law from Delaware to '[Insert Company's State]', directly contradicts Section 9. Section 9 states that the agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties. However, changing the governing law introduces the laws of '[Insert Company's State]', potentially affecting the interpretation and enforceability of the agreement, thus contradicting the 'entire agreement' clause in Section 9. This is a strong contradiction because it introduces uncertainty and legal implications based on the altered governing law.

perturbation.original_text: 9. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreemen t of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and can cels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the Parties hereto relating to the Proprietary Information, whether written or oral, expressed or implied. The Parties agree that this Agreement is severable and that in the event any provision of this Agreement is held to be ille gal, invalid or enforceab le, the legality, validity and enforceability of the remaining provisi ons will not be affected or impaired.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. This Agreement constitutes the entire agreemen t of the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes and can cels all prior communications, understandings and agreements between the Parties hereto relating to the Proprietary Information, whether written or oral, expressed or implied. The Parties agree that this Agreement is not severable and that in the event any provision of this Agreement is held to be ille gal, invalid or enforceab le, the legality, validity and enforceability of the remaining provisi ons will be affected or impaired.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'this Agreement is severable' to 'this Agreement is not severable,' a contradiction is introduced because the agreement implies in previous sections, specifically sections 2 and 7, that certain provisions (confidentiality, remedies) are more critical than others. This means that if a less important clause is deemed illegal, the entire agreement becomes invalid, undermining the intent of preserving the core confidentiality obligations. This goes against the other sections, where the intent is to keep the confidentiality. The contradiction is in section 2, due to the confidentiality intent, but section 9, goes against it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Proprietary Information shall be held in stri ct confidence by Receiving Party and shall not be used by Receiving Party except for (i) the pur poses described in this Agreement. Such consent to disclose Proprietary Information to employees of Receiving Party or its affiliated company (“affiliated Receiving Party”) with a legitimate “need to know” and only for the purposes described in this Agreement is herewith given, but further consent shall be required for disclosure to others or authorization of use by others. For purposes of this Confidentiality Agreement, an "affiliated Receiving Party" mean s and includes a parent, if any, of Receiving Party and all present and future companies in whic h Receiving Party, or its parent, individually or collectively, directly or indirectly, through one or more intermediaries, owns or controls fifty percent (50%) or more of the outstanding stock having the right to vote for or appoint directors thereof. Prior to any use or disclosure of Propr ietary Information by or to another, Receiving Party shall ensure that the re cipient has entered into an agreement limiting the recipient's disclosure and use of Proprietary Informa tion consistent with this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 9 modifies the severability clause from 'severable' to 'not severable.' This directly contradicts the intent of Section 2, which emphasizes the importance of maintaining confidentiality. If the entire agreement is not severable, the invalidation of even a minor clause could jeopardize the core confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 2, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
